# IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:) | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ IOBluetooth ](/documentation/iobluetooth)

- [ IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:) ](/documentation/IOBluetooth/IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:))

-  IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:) 

Function# IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:)

macOS

```
func IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(
    _ inName: String!,
    _ inPath: String!
) -> String!
```

## [ Parameters ](/documentation/IOBluetooth/IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:)#parameters)

`inName`Name of file that needs unique name in the specified path.

`inPath`Path you are trying to put file into.

## [Return Value](/documentation/IOBluetooth/IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:)#return-value)

String with a unique name appended on it for the provided path.

## [Discussion](/documentation/IOBluetooth/IOBluetoothGetUniqueFileNameAndPath(_:_:)#Discussion)

When passed a VALID filename and a VALID path, this routine will return you a the path with the name appended onto it. If it already exist, it will insert a #1, #2, etc. Example: If you pass @“TestFile.txt” and @”~/Documents”, you will get @“~Documents/TestFile.txt”. If one already exists, you will be returned: @“~Documents/TestFile #1.txt”.
